rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS E. WILSON VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:00 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued on the court¿s own motion to October 23, 2013 at 9:00a.m. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Defendant Cbs Corporation'S (Fka Viacom Inc., Fka Westinghouse Electric Corporation) Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:00 A.M., LINE 2, Plaintiffs¿ motion to tax defendant CBS Corp.¿s memorandum of costs is granted in part and denied in part. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs is taxed $1144 for costs related to pro hac vice admissions because defendant failed to demonstrate that such costs were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation. Defendant¿s memorandum of costs is taxed $9,653.50 for costs identified as ¿Custodian of Records for PG&E¿ because it is either disallowed under C.C.P. 1033.5(b) or even if not explicitly disallowed defendant failed to demonstrate that such cost was reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation. Plaintiffs¿ motion is denied as to the RecordTrak charges as such charges are allowed and were incurred, reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient or beneficial to its preparation, and reasonable in amount. Plaintiffs have withdrawn their motion as to all other items. If a hearing is requested, it will be 9:00a.m. Due to the court¿s trial schedule all motion hearings must conclude no later than 9:25a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
